# X2Chess Architecture ADR Manual

Last updated: 2026-05-03

## Purpose

This manual contains Architecture Decision Records (ADRs): why major architectural choices were made and their implications.

## ADR-001: Fat-client-first browser runtime
- Status: Accepted
- Date: 2026-03-18
- Decision:
  - The primary/full runtime is a browser-based fat client executed locally.
  - Client behavior and core interaction logic remain client-side first.
- Implication:
  - New features must be evaluated first for local browser execution.

## ADR-002: Remote-launch, local-execution delivery
- Status: Accepted
- Date: 2026-03-18
- Decision:
  - Client assets may be launched/delivered from remote servers.
  - Execution remains local in browser/app runtime; backend does not own UI execution flow.
- Implication:
  - Deployment flexibility is improved without server-side UI lock-in.

## ADR-003: Resource model combines local and remote sources
- Status: Accepted
- Date: 2026-03-18
- Decision:
  - The client uses local resources (storage/cache/local processing) as first-class capabilities.
  - The client integrates remote resources through explicit adapters/integration boundaries.
- Implication:
  - Resource access remains composable and environment-aware.

## ADR-004: Full client and reduced app-profile from one codebase
- Status: Accepted
- Date: 2026-03-18
- Decision:
  - Keep one shared UI/domain codebase in `frontend/src`.
  - Deliver a full fat-client browser profile and a reduced app-profile via Tauri shell.
- Implication:
  - Feature boundaries between full and reduced profiles must be explicit.

## ADR-005: PGN model as canonical editor state
- Status: Accepted
- Date: 2026-03-18
- Decision:
  - Parse text into a model (`parsePgnToModel`) and serialize it back (`serializeModelToPgn`).
  - Editing commands operate on the model first, then sync UI.
- Implication:
  - Model transformation quality is central for correctness and undo/redo behavior.

## ADR-006: Visual asset resilience strategy
- Status: Accepted
- Date: 2026-03-18
- Decision:
  - Attempt remote asset hydration first.
  - Fall back to local cache or bundled local assets.
- Implication:
  - Full and reduced profiles remain visually functional even without network.

## ADR-007: Component-Contract required for components
- Status: Accepted
- Date: 2026-03-18
- Decision:
  - Components, especially GUI components, must expose an identifiable Integration API, Configuration API, and Communication API.
  - Public API behavior must be documented in code-level public documentation.
- Implication:
  - Component integration and evolution remain explicit, testable, and maintainable.

## ADR-008: Professional default CSS with user-adaptable theming
- Status: Accepted
- Date: 2026-03-18
- Decision:
  - The default UI style must remain clean and professional.
  - CSS architecture must support future user adaptation through explicit theming extension points (tokens/CSS variables or equivalent).
- Implication:
  - Visual consistency is preserved while allowing controlled personalization.

## ADR-009: Scenario-based local runtime data root
- Status: Accepted
- Date: 2026-03-18
- Decision:
  - Store local runtime data under top-level `run/` for `DEV` and `TEST`.
  - Production (`PROD`) resolves to explicit production data root (`X2CHESS_PROD_DATA_ROOT`), or OS app-data default when not set.
  - Development defaults to `run/DEV` and scenario can be switched via `X2CHESS_RUN_SCENARIO`.
- Implication:
  - Local runs remain organized while production avoids repo-relative data coupling.

## ADR-010: Client-owned game file library with autosave
- Status: Accepted
- Date: 2026-03-18
- Decision:
  - Active game files are loaded from `run/<SCENARIO>/games`.
  - UI exposes game selection dropdown and autosaves edits back to selected file.
  - Resource loading/saving is implemented in client runtime via local file APIs (folder selection + file handles).
- Implication:
  - Editing workflow remains file-backed and scenario-aware without backend mediation.

## ADR-011: Layered runtime configuration with partial user overrides
- Status: Accepted
- Date: 2026-03-18
- Decision:
  - Keep one default config bundled in client code.
  - Allow user overrides as partial config in active data area (`config/user-config.json`).
  - Resolve effective config by deep-merge of default + user override.
  - Start with text editor settings in this runtime config contract.
- Implication:
  - Defaults remain stable while users can personalize behavior entirely through client-owned local data.

## ADR-012: Top-level resource library ownership and canonical kinds
- Status: Accepted
- Date: 2026-03-20
- Decision:
  - Keep `resource/` as an independent top-level library for PGN resource domain contracts and client dispatch.
  - Canonical resource kinds are `file`, `directory`, and `db`; frontend uses compatibility mapping only as a transition aid.
  - Keep SQL schema and migration ownership inside `resource/database/`, not in frontend modules.
  - Keep metadata schema centralized in `resource/domain` and have frontend consumers reuse canonical constants/contracts.
- Implication:
  - Architecture boundaries remain explicit and prevent drift of resource/domain logic into frontend-only modules.

## ADR-013: React as sole frontend runtime
- Status: Accepted
- Date: 2026-03-21
- Decision:
  - React owns all rendering, state, and event handling in the frontend.
  - The single `useReducer` store (`AppStoreState`) is the sole source of UI state truth; all mutations via `dispatch(action)`.
  - All service initialisation is owned by the `useAppStartup` hook; stable service callbacks are exposed exclusively via `ServiceContext` / `useServiceContext()`.
  - Components read state directly from context.
  - `src/runtime/bootstrap_shared.ts` provides `SourceRefLike`, `SessionLike`, `EMPTY_GAME_PGN`, and `isLikelyPgnText` consumed by pure-logic modules (`session_store`, `session_persistence`, `resources/index`, `resources_viewer/index`).
  - Pure-logic modules (`model/`, `editor/`, `board/`, `game_sessions/`, `resources/`) remain framework-free and are imported directly by hooks and components.
- Implication:
  - Component lifecycle governs all UI concerns.
  - The Tauri profile and the browser fat-client share the same React rendering path without divergence.
  - There is one state model and one service initialisation point.

## ADR-014: Resource-domain events for mutation propagation
- Status: Accepted
- Date: 2026-04-20
- Decision:
  - Introduce a typed in-memory resource event hub in `frontend/src/core/events/resource_domain_events.ts`.
  - Services emit environment-agnostic resource mutation events (`resource.gameCreated`, `resource.gameSaved`, `resource.gameReordered`, normalized `resource.resourceChanged`).
  - React consumers subscribe via hooks/effects and update UI state for matching resources (`kind` + `locator`) instead of relying on row-count heuristics or ad hoc callback chains.
- Implication:
  - Resource freshness behavior becomes explicit and reusable across resource table, explorer, and search surfaces.
  - Service modules remain framework-independent and do not require DOM event channels.
  - Regressions from duplicated cache synchronization paths are reduced.

## ADR-015: Open-from-resource hydrates PGN headers (no parallel session metadata map)
- Status: Accepted
- Date: 2026-05-02
- Decision:
  - Session game rendering and save/export read metadata only from `PgnModel` headers (plus movetext), not from a second `resourceMetadataSnapshot` on `GameSession`.
  - When loading a game from a resource, list-row metadata is merged into the model with `hydratePgnModelFromResourceMetadata` for keys where the loaded PGN tag is missing or blank; non-blank PGN values stay authoritative.
  - Database `game_metadata` remains a derived index of tags extracted from stored PGN on save (existing adapter behavior).
- Implication:
  - One consistent header map for GRP, tabs, and file export; no duplicate merge at render time.

## ADR-016: `.x2chess` derived indexes, title hint on save, and Tauri DB gateway serialization
- Status: Accepted
- Date: 2026-05-02
- Decision:
  - Rebuilds of `game_metadata`, `position_hashes`, and `move_edges` for a game go only through `rewriteDerivedGameIndexes` in the same SQLite transaction as the `games` row write (`parts/resource/src/adapters/db/`).
  - On `save`, `games.title_hint` is recomputed from bracket headers (same derivation as list titles) so `load` stays aligned with stored PGN; `create` continues to store the caller-supplied title hint (session disambiguation suffixes).
  - The Tauri `DbGateway` serializes `query`/`execute`/`transaction` IPC per database file path and runs transaction bodies on a **direct** inner gateway so nested `execute` calls do not deadlock the queue.
- Implication:
  - One obvious write pipeline for derived tables; reduced risk of interleaved transactions on the shared Rust SQLite connection.

## ADR-017: Tauri command IPC through a TypeScript bridge
- Status: Accepted
- Date: 2026-05-03
- Decision:
  - Application UI and domain-adjacent TypeScript must not call Rust commands via scattered `@tauri-apps/api/core` `invoke` imports or ad hoc `window.__TAURI__` reads.
  - Generic IPC uses **`frontend/src/platform/desktop/tauri_ipc_bridge.ts`** (`tauriInvoke`, `isTauri`). Higher-level access continues through **`frontend/src/platform/desktop/tauri/tauri_gateways.ts`** and resource adapters that already encapsulate `invoke` (`picker_fs_helpers`, `runtime_config_service`).
  - Exceptions for non-invoke Tauri surfaces (logging plugin, native menu API, updater/relaunch, narrow helpers) are listed in **`dev/rules/tauri-ipc-bridge.mdc`** and kept minimal.
  - **`parts/engines`** stays shell-agnostic; the Tauri-backed **`EngineProcess`** implementation is **`frontend/src/platform/desktop/tauri_engine_adapter.ts`** (uses **`tauri_ipc_bridge`** for `invoke`).
- Implication:
  - Tauri v2 webviews that expose only `__TAURI_INTERNALS__` remain compatible; feature code stays testable and isolated from Tauri package churn.
  - Shared **`parts/`** packages avoid `@tauri-apps/*`; desktop-only glue remains under **`frontend/src/platform/desktop/`**.

## Change Log

- 2026-03-18:
  - Initial ADR set captured from architecture evolution history.
  - Includes runtime/storage, UI/component, delivery, and config decisions through ADR-011.
  - Updated ADR-010 and ADR-011 to enforce client-owned local resource handling.
- 2026-03-20:
  - Added ADR-012: top-level resource library ownership and canonical kinds.
- 2026-03-21:
  - Added ADR-013: React as sole frontend runtime.
- 2026-04-20:
  - Added ADR-014: typed resource-domain events for mutation propagation.
- 2026-05-02:
  - Added ADR-015: open-from-resource PGN header hydration; single source for session metadata.
  - Added ADR-016: `.x2chess` derived-index pipeline, `title_hint` refresh on save, Tauri DB gateway queue.
- 2026-05-03:
  - Added ADR-017: Tauri `invoke` IPC boundary (`tauri_ipc_bridge`, governed by `dev/rules/tauri-ipc-bridge.mdc`).
